(born 29 October 1973) is a [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxing "Professional boxing"). He held the [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") super featherweight title twice between 2007 and 2010, and challenged twice for other world titles.

first_paragraph: **Mzonke Fana** (born 29 October 1973) is a [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxing "Professional boxing"). He held the [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") super featherweight title twice between 2007 and 2010, and challenged twice for other world titles.

**Mzonke Fana** (born 29 October 1973) is a [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxing "Professional boxing"). He held the [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") super featherweight title twice between 2007 and 2010, and challenged twice for other world titles.

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mzonke_Fana&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Professional career")]

### Super featherweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mzonke_Fana&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Super featherweight")]

Known as *"The Rose of Khayelitsha"*, he was a virtual unknown in the boxing world until he challenged and defeated [Malcolm Klassen](/wiki/Malcolm_Klassen "Malcolm Klassen") for the [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") Super featherweight title in 2007. Prior to that victory, he was knocked out on 9 April 2005 by future Hall of Famer [Marco Antonio Barrera](/wiki/Marco_Antonio_Barrera "Marco Antonio Barrera") in the second round for the [WBC](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council") Super featherweight title in his first world title challenge.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

### Lightweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mzonke_Fana&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Lightweight")]

On 7 December 2013, he fought upcoming fighter [Edis Tatli](/wiki/Edis_Tatli "Edis Tatli") for the [WBA](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association") Inter-Continental Lightweight Title at the Barona Areena in Espoo, [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), but lost via unanimous decision. He fought Terry Flanagan for the [WBO](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization") Lightweight Title and lost via unanimous decision.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
